description: The vector class holds a single 3 component vector. A vector describes a direction in space, and it's important to use a vector or Position as appropriate for the data that is being calculated. When being multiplied by a Matrix, this class will implicitly have a 4th component (W component) of 0. A new vector can be created without any arguments, with 3 arguments for the x,y,z values, or with a single argument which is a variable that has 3 entries such as a list of length 3, or a position or vector. Note: tdu.___ and TDU.___ can be used interchangeably. In general, TDU.___ is used to represent a class, while tdu.___ is used for the instantiator function. Examples of creating a vector:

members.name: x
members.description: Gets or sets the X component of the vector.

members.name: y
members.description: Gets or sets the Y component of the vector.

members.name: z
members.description: Gets or sets the Z component of the vector.

methods.name: angle
methods.description: Returns the angel (in degrees) between the current vector and specified vector (vec).

methods.name: scale
methods.description: Scales each component of the vector by the specified values. *x, y, z - The values to scale each component of the vector by.

methods.name: normalize
methods.description: Makes the length of this vector 1.

methods.name: length
methods.description: Returns the length of this vector.

methods.name: lengthSquared
methods.description: Returns the squared length of this vector.

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Returns a new vector that is a copy of the vector.

methods.name: distance
methods.description: Returns the distance of the current vector to specified vector (vec).

methods.name: lerp
methods.description: Returns the linear interpolation of this vector and vec2. That is vec1 * (1.0 - t) + vec2 * t, where vec1 is the current vector. The value for t is not restricted to the range [0, 1].

methods.name: slerp
methods.description: Returns the spherical interpolation of this vector and vec2. The value for t is not restricted to the range [0, 1].

methods.name: dot
methods.description: Returns the dot product of this vector and the passed vector. *vec - The other vector to use to calculate the dot product

methods.name: cross
methods.description: Returns the cross product of this vector and the passed vector. The operation is self cross vec. *vec - The other vector to use to calculate the cross product.

methods.name: project
methods.description: Projects this vector onto the plan defined by vec1 and vec2. Both vec1 and vec2 must be normalized. The result may not be normalized. *vec1, vec2 - The vectors that specify the plane to project onto. Must be normalized.

methods.name: reflect
methods.description: Reflects the current vector about the specified vector (vec).

_provenance.fact: scraped from the Vector Class wiki page
